nodes.x: Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me when I was in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
Hear, O Lord, when I cry with my voice: have mercy also upon me, and answer me.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O Lord.
Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy oracle.
O ye sons of men, how long will ye turn my glory into shame? how long will ye love vanity, and seek after leasing? Selah.
How long will ye judge unjustly, and accept the persons of the wicked? Selah.

And now my beloved brethren, I say unto you, can ye withstand these sayings; yea, can ye lay aside these things, and trample the Holy One under your feet; yea, can ye be puffed up in the pride of your hearts; yea, will ye still persist in the wearing of costly apparel and setting your hearts upon the vain things of the world, upon your riches?
The days of his youth hast thou shortened: thou hast covered him with shame. Selah.
The women of my people have ye cast out from their pleasant houses; from their children have ye taken away my glory for ever.
O Jerusalem, wash thine heart from wickedness, that thou mayest be saved. How long shall thy vain thoughts lodge within thee?
But know that the Lord hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the Lord will hear when I call unto him.
Verily I say unto you, even now, if he will hearken unto my voice, it shall be well with him. Even so. Amen.
Therefore thus saith the Lord God; As I live, surely mine oath that he hath despised, and my covenant that he hath broken, even it will I recompense upon his own head.
Because he hath inclined his ear unto me, therefore will I call upon him as long as I live.
The Lord is nigh unto all them that call upon him, to all that call upon him in truth.

And now I say unto you that the good shepherd doth call after you; and if you will hearken unto his voice he will bring you into his fold, and ye are his sheep; and he commandeth you that ye suffer no ravenous wolf to enter among you, that ye may not be destroyed.
Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.

See that ye love one snothet; cease to be covetous, learn to impart one to another as the gospel requires; cease to be idle, cease to be unclean; ceate to find fault one with another; cease to sleep longer than is neidlul; retire to thy bed early, that ye may not be weary; arise early, that your bod cs and y our minds may be invigorated: a; d above all things, clothe your* selves with the bonds…
Salvation belongeth unto the Lord: thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.
Remember all thy offerings, and accept thy burnt sacrifice; Selah.
God be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.
Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the Lord.
Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.

Keep the commandments of the Lord; observe the instructions and counsel you receive from those placed over you to preside; be faithful and industrious, economienl and prudent; sock continually unto the Lord for wisdom, and train up your children in His nurture and admonition, that when wo shall have finished our pilgrimage upon the earth, we may go hence in peace, having wrought; righteousness…

Go trusting in God, and continue to trust in him, and he will open
your way and multiply blessings upon you, and your souls will be
satisfied with his goodness. I cannot promise you any good in taking an
unrighteous course; your lives must be examples of good works. 8:73.
Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the Lord.
There be many that say, Who will shew us any good? Lord, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.
And thou saidst, I will surely do thee good, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.
And there are none that doeth good except those who are ready to receive the fulness of my gospel, which I have sent forth unto this generation.
There is one come out of thee, that imagineth evil against the Lord, a wicked counsellor.

And wo be unto the children of men if this be the case; for there shall be none that doeth good among you, no not one. For if there be one among you that doeth good, he shall work by the power and gifts of God.
Thou hast put gladness in my heart, more than in the time that their corn and their wine increased.
Behold, canst thou read this without rejoicing and lifting up thy heart for gladness?
For thou hast made him most blessed for ever: thou hast made him exceeding glad with thy countenance.
Because thou servedst not the Lord thy God with joyfulness, and with gladness of heart, for the abundance of all things;

And joy and gladness is taken from the plentiful field, and from the land of Moab; and I have caused wine to fail from the winepresses: none shall tread with shouting; their shouting shall be no shouting.
Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, Lord, only makest me dwell in safety.
When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.
For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.

Wherefore, if ye have slept in peace blessed are you; for as you now behold me and know that I am, even so shall ye come unto me and your souls shall live, and your redemption shall be perfected; and the saints shall come forth from the four quarters of the earth.
And thy life shall hang in doubt before thee; and thou shalt fear day and night, and shalt have none assurance of thy life:
Hold thou me up, and I shall be safe: and I will have respect unto thy statutes continually.
